verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is an IZUMO family member, but unlike IZUMO1 (the sperm-egg fusion protein with a TM domain displayed on sperm surface), IZUMO4 lacks a canonical transmembrane domain and is reported to localize to the nucleus. The IZUMO family includes both surface (IZUMO1) and non-surface members; IZUMO4 falls in the latter category. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program documented. (2) No ectodomain-shedding or TM precursor biology reported. (3) No wash-resistant TM-partner tethering documented. (4) No minority PM pool from cycling or exocytosis documented. (5) No cell-death/cancer/activation-induced outer-leaflet display reported; no germline/tissue-restricted surface display documented; not a lysosomal TM protein; no dual-localization PM pool; no stable TM-partner surface anchoring. Nuclear localization is the consistent annotation with no surface evidence for this specific paralog.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO1 is a germline/sperm surface protein; IZUMO4 paralogy could hint at tissue-restricted surface biology, but gene-specific evidence points to nuclear localization with no documented surface pool.
